key_insight: Graph neural networks are now a general-purpose choice for relational data, but their real value depends on whether graph structure justifies the added computational cost.
engineering_relevance: For materials discovery, the paper frames GNNs as a design-and-selection problem: choose graph construction and architecture based on expressiveness, scalability, and deployment constraints rather than leaderboard performance alone.
method: Unifies spectral and spatial GNN formulations under shared principles
Links model expressiveness to the Weisfeiler-Leman hierarchy
Compares 12 application domains with attention to graph construction, costs, and dominant architectures
evidence_signal: The survey synthesizes cross-domain findings from reported benchmark results and explicitly separates genuine gains from weak baselines, favorable splits, and deployment gaps.
watch_item: Heterophily, temporal dynamics, and distribution shift can erase benchmark gains, so strong public scores do not guarantee real-world robustness.
paper_title: Graph Neural Networks Applications Across Domains: All Insights You Need
